info.name: Exposed JSON Configuration Files
author: geeknik
info.description: Detects exposed JSON configuration files containing sensitive information including API keys, access tokens, AWS credentials, database configurations, base URLs, file paths, and application settings. These files often contain production configurations and credentials that should not be publicly accessible.

request:
GET //config.json HTTP/1.1
Host: 127.0.0.1:10981
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/114.0
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Connection: close
Content-Length: 282
Accept-Ranges: bytes
Content-Type: application/json
Date: Fri, 13 Mar 2026 22:16:59 GMT
Etag: "11a-6482e6d9bd3c3"
Last-Modified: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 10:35:04 GMT
Server: Apache/2.4.66 (Debian)

{
    "database_host": "localhost",
    "database_user": "admin",
    "database_password": "SuperSecretPassword123",
    "api_key": "sk_live_abcdef1234567890",
    "smtp_server": "smtp.example.com",
    "smtp_user": "mailer@example.com",
    "smtp_password": "MailSecret!"
}
curl-command: curl -X 'GET' -d '' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/114.0' 'http://127.0.0.1:10981//config.json'

info.name: Cookies without HttpOnly attribute - Detect
author: rj coleman
mr.bobo hp
info.description: Checks whether cookies in the HTTP response contain the HttpOnly attribute. If the HttpOnly flag is set, it means that the cookie is HTTP-only
info.impact: Lack of HttpOnly flag on cookies increases the risk of client-side attacks, compromising user session security and data privacy.
info.remediation: Ensure that all cookies are set with the HttpOnly attribute to prevent client-side access.

response: [authenticated]

info.name: Cookies without HttpOnly attribute - Detect
author: rj coleman
mr.bobo hp
info.description: Checks whether cookies in the HTTP response contain the HttpOnly attribute. If the HttpOnly flag is set, it means that the cookie is HTTP-only

info.remediation: Ensure that all cookies are set with the HttpOnly attribute to prevent client-side access.

response: [PHPSESSID]

info.name: Cookies without HttpOnly attribute - Detect
author: rj coleman
mr.bobo hp
info.description: Checks whether cookies in the HTTP response contain the HttpOnly attribute. If the HttpOnly flag is set, it means that the cookie is HTTP-only
info.impact: Lack of HttpOnly flag on cookies increases the risk of client-side attacks, compromising user session security and data privacy.

response: [authenticated]

info.name: Cookies without Secure attribute - Detect
author: vthiery
rj coleman
info.description: Checks whether cookies in the HTTP response contain the Secure attribute. If the Secure flag is set, it means that the cookie can only be transmitted over HTTPS
info.impact: Lack of Secure flag on cookies allows the cookie to be sent over unsecure HTTP, making it vulnerable to man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks.
info.remediation: Ensure that all cookies are set with the Secure attribute to prevent MITM attacks.

response: [PHPSESSID]

response: [connect.sid]

author: dwisiswant0

info.name: Fuzzing Parameters - Cross-Site Scripting
author: kazet
info.description: Cross-site scripting was discovered via a search for reflected parameter values in the server response via GET-requests.

request:
